protocol: baryonic-only prediction, SHA-256 sealed before Vobs revealed; no per-galaxy tuning
sample: SPARC curated: Q<=2 & Inc>=30deg & e_Vobs<0.1*Vobs  (galaxies=147, points=2696)
verdict: QLF (0 free params) reproduces the SPARC rotation curves at the observational-floor scatter (~0.13 dex), statistically identical to best-fit MOND, and far better than Newton (no dark matter). NFW fits tighter (~0.06 dex) but only with 294 free parameters (2/galaxy) — absorbing the per-galaxy scatter that the RAR's observed tightness shows is not physical halo diversity. Parameter economy: QLF 0, NFW 294, for the same data.
